name: Car Engine
summary: A simplified internal combustion engine with core parts for exploration, ARIA explanation, and what-if simulation.

components.name: Engine Block
components.function: Holds cylinders, coolant passages, oil channels, and the structural body of the engine.
components.riskIfRemoved: The engine cannot exist structurally without the block.

microLevels.name: Aluminum alloy grain structure
microLevels.description: A microscopic view of alloy grains that affect strength and heat transfer.

components.name: Piston
components.function: Compresses the air-fuel mixture and converts combustion pressure into downward motion.
components.riskIfRemoved: The cylinder loses compression and the engine cannot produce useful power.

microLevels.name: Aluminum alloy
microLevels.description: Light metal alloy chosen for low mass and good heat transfer.

microLevels.name: Aluminum atom
microLevels.description: Atomic-scale placeholder for future material exploration.

components.name: Crankshaft
components.function: Converts piston up-down motion into rotating motion for the drivetrain.
components.riskIfRemoved: The engine cannot transmit piston force into rotation.

microLevels.name: Steel crystal structure
microLevels.description: Steel microstructure affects fatigue resistance and torque capacity.

components.name: Intake Valve
components.function: Lets fresh air-fuel mixture enter the cylinder at the correct timing.
components.riskIfRemoved: Airflow timing fails, causing misfire or no combustion.

components.name: Spark Plug
components.function: Creates an electric spark that ignites the compressed air-fuel mixture.
components.riskIfRemoved: Fuel may not ignite, so the cylinder stops producing power.

components.name: Cooling Fan
components.function: Moves air through the radiator area to help remove heat from coolant.
components.riskIfRemoved: Heat rises quickly during low-speed operation and may damage engine parts.
